sources.citation: Fan, Xiaolei. 2026. The Modal Meaning of the jau5 (有) have+VP in Hong Kong Cantonese. Journal of Chinese Linguistics 54(2): 458-479.
sources.publication_type: peer-reviewed journal article; official journal abstract and author publication metadata
sources.supports: Fan describes nonpossessive auxiliary/modal 有 + dynamic VP and reports preference in polar questions, conditionals, and negatives.
sources.limitation: Do not infer full syntactic details, unrestricted VP selection, or exact question templates from an abstract.

sources.citation: Hara, Yurie. 2023. Cantonese Question Particles. In Discourse Particles in Asian Languages, Volume I: East Asia. Routledge.
sources.publication_type: peer-reviewed academic book chapter; author full-text manuscript and publisher metadata
sources.locator: author PDF pp. 1-2 and 13-17; Figure 2 and conclusion
sources.supports: Hara’s experiments favor A-not-A in neutral contexts, while the discussion allows cases where private belief is not overtly expressed.
sources.limitation: Do not encode “neutral only” as an absolute grammar rule or infer speaker beliefs from surface form alone.

sources.citation: Law, Ann. 2001. A-not-A Questions in Cantonese. UCL Working Papers in Linguistics 13: 295-318.

sources.limitation: Do not reject a structurally valid A-not-A question solely because discourse bias is possible.

sources.citation: Li, Clare. 2017. The Syntactic and Pragmatic Properties of A-not-A Question in Chinese. MA thesis, University of Canterbury.
sources.publication_type: university MA thesis with full repository text
sources.locator: PDF pp. 50-51 and 86-87; examples 可唔可以 and 方唔方便
sources.supports: The film sample attests a-not-AB and a-not-A patterns in which only the first syllable of a disyllabic predicate occurs in the affirmative arm.
sources.limitation: Do not generate arbitrary truncations; the source does not establish a free syllable-deletion algorithm for every disyllabic item.

embedded.language_claim: Published Cantonese analyses and independently sourced examples attest V + 完 + 咗 + object ordering, with 完 inside the perfective scope; exact lexical, discourse, and speaker-variation boundaries remain research-pending.
embedded.parser_representation_decision: Canto Span represents V完 as an inner CompletionVP and 咗 plus the overt object as an outer PerfectiveVP. This scope representation is an engineering analysis aligned with published inner/outer-aspect accounts, not native-speaker analysis validation.
embedded.parser_heuristic: When visible action + 完 + 咗 + overt-object cues occur, the parser composes an inner completion predicate under outer perfective aspect. The heuristic must remain review-bearing for native-rejected surfaces and incompatible aspect orders.

accounting.note: External mapping is retained; exact productivity still depends on pattern-specific evidence and acceptance.

claims.promotion_blocker: legacy claim was not demonstrably derived from the recorded sources; reconstruct the claim from screened propositions first
claims.next_research_form: Which externally documented Cantonese constructions, if any, justify the scope and boundaries currently represented by CompletionVP?

embedded.language_claim: Cantonese conventionally permits 用嚟/用來 to relate an overt object, resource, or artifact to a stated use or function; intention, affordance, actual-use-event, subject/topic status, omitted-topic licensing, and broader alternative-marker boundaries remain provisional.
embedded.parser_representation_decision: Canto Span represents the narrow supported relation with one IntendedFunctionRelation node. Direct resource NPs are not forced into a public Topic analysis; a Topic child is retained only when a separate overt user supports left dislocation. 用, 嚟/來, predicate material, controls, and particles remain transparent.
embedded.parser_heuristic: The parser selects IntendedFunctionRelation only when an overt non-person function-bearing resource precedes adjacent 用 + 嚟/來 and visible predicate material. Modal readings and perfective action material remain review-bearing. Lone-user, bare-linker, aspect-interrupted, A-not-A, reduplicated 用來用去, coordination, lexical-use, instrumental-use, and directional-motion environments remain outside this narrow replacement or blocked.

accounting.note: Prior provisional status withdrawn until at least one exact source claim and one-speaker positive/negative review satisfy the current provisional checklist. Provisional mapping requires retroactive source re-verification and two-speaker review before productive acceptance. External mapping is retained; exact productivity still depends on pattern-specific evidence and acceptance.

embedded.language_claim: Lexical Cantonese 畀/俾 can occur with two overt postverbal nominal spans; the reviewed theme-before-person profile supports bounded candidate roles, while person-before-thing order remains role-unresolved and speaker-variable.
embedded.parser_representation_decision: Canto Span uses LexicalGiveRelation for lexical GIVE without asserting a ditransitive VP category; only the reviewed baseline exports Theme/Object and recipient-candidate roles.
embedded.parser_heuristic: Require lexical GIVE, optional immediate 咗, exactly two uniquely split overt nominal spans, no later predicate or second marker, and no hidden arguments; nonbaseline order exports participant slots only.

accounting.note: Prior provisional status withdrawn until at least one exact source claim and one-speaker positive/negative review satisfy the current provisional checklist. Provisional mapping requires retroactive source re-verification and two-speaker review before productive acceptance. External mapping is retained; exact productivity still depends on pattern-specific evidence and acceptance.

reaudit.next_action: Reverify at least one exact source, confirm one independent native-speaker review of the relevant positive and negative examples, and draft boundaries before restoring provisional status.

authority.limitations: Prior provisional status withdrawn until the current provisional checklist is reverified. One Guangzhou speaker supplied surface judgments only; alternative order is mixed; no native structural-analysis or human-expert validation; source claims do not establish free alternation or a weight threshold.

embedded.language_claim: Reviewed sources and natural examples support overt demonstrative–classifier–noun sequences. Exact productivity, classifier compatibility, and boundaries remain research pending; no active DEMO01 promotion candidate exists.
embedded.parser_representation_decision: Canto Span may represent an overt demonstrative, compatible classifier, and overt noun head as OvertHeadDemonstrativeClassifierNP inside the shared NP subsystem. Headless, unknown-head, classifier-incompatible, and unresolved attachment cases cannot become licensed NP inputs.
embedded.parser_heuristic: Select only when three overt adjacent components are recognized as demonstrative, classifier, and complete noun head, and the classifier–head lexical compatibility class is verified. Insert no hidden numeral or noun. This runtime structure is not a productive-status claim.

accounting.note: DEMO01 is abandoned. Prior sources remain provenance for a research-pending structural component; no render or held-out promotion claim is active.

reaudit.status_reason: DEMO01 promotion track abandoned; shared NP component remains research_pending
reaudit.productive_blockers: all Definition of Done linguistic gates; no active promotion candidate; second-speaker work frozen
reaudit.next_action: No DEMO01 action. Maintain the component through shared NP-subsystem tests; reopen linguistic promotion only by explicit future decision.

authority.limitations: DEMO01 promotion, render, and held-out work was abandoned in v0.5.184. Prior evidence remains provenance for a research-pending shared NP component only. Classifier-head compatibility is bounded; no active promotion candidate or held-out claim remains.

claims.next_research_form: Which externally documented Cantonese constructions, if any, justify the scope and boundaries currently represented by OvertHeadDemonstrativeClassifierNP?

sources.citation: Bond, Francis and Joanna Ut-Seong Sio. 2024. A Construction-based Approach to Cantonese Classifiers. Proceedings of the 31st International Conference on Head-Driven Phrase Structure Grammar, 60-75.
sources.publication_type: peer-reviewed conference paper with implemented grammar
sources.supports: Four Cantonese NP schemata D-(X)-C-N, X-C-N, C-N, and N; classifier cardinality and cognitive-status distinctions; implemented HPSG analysis.
sources.limitation: The HPSG analysis is one explicit formalization, not a settled universal ontology; its semantic type hierarchy should not be imported wholesale into Canto Span.

sources.citation: Lam, Charles, Chaak Ming Lau, and Jackson L. Lee. 2024. Multi-Tiered Cantonese Word Segmentation. Proceedings of LREC-COLING 2024, 11993-12002.
sources.publication_type: peer-reviewed conference paper and corpus annotation scheme
sources.locator: pp. 11997-11999; PDF p. 5 section 4.4.3 examples 第-一 and 第-二-隻; classifier/NP and noun-ellipsis sections; PDF pp. 5-6 sections 4.5.2 and 4.6
sources.supports: Overt segmentation of classifier NPs and noun ellipsis; ordinals with 第 treated as determiners, including classifier-headed noun ellipsis; distinction between verbal possessive 有 and existential-marker 有; degree modifiers and opaque lexical adjective units.
sources.limitation: The paper specifies a word-segmentation annotation scheme rather than a complete syntax or semantics. Its segmentation decisions corroborate overt boundaries but do not establish all ordinal readings, argument structures, ellipsis licensing, or parser nodes.

embedded.language_claim: Cantonese 畀/俾 sequences can realize canonical passive, retained-object indirect passive, or permissive let/allow readings; some NP1 畀 NP2 VP strings remain contextually ambiguous.
embedded.parser_representation_decision: Canto Span uses one transparent PassivePermissiveRelation with trace-level reading candidates and subtype cues rather than the debunked generic AffectednessFrame.
embedded.parser_heuristic: The parser requires an overt pre-marker participant, overt post-marker participant, and predicate material; it preserves passive/permissive ambiguity and does not treat negated, aspect-marked, A-not-A, transfer, beneficiary, agent-omitted, or fragment strings as clean passive relations.

accounting.note: Prior provisional status withdrawn until at least one exact source claim and one-speaker positive/negative review satisfy the current provisional checklist. Provisional mapping requires retroactive source re-verification and two-speaker review before productive acceptance. External mapping is retained; exact productivity still depends on pattern-specific evidence and acceptance.

reaudit.next_action: Reverify at least one exact source, confirm one independent native-speaker review of the relevant positive and negative examples, and draft boundaries before restoring provisional status.

sources.limitation: Do not treat a bare numeral as equivalent, invent an antecedent, or conflate noun ellipsis with a full overt-head NP.

embedded.language_claim: Reviewed sources attest direct resource + 用嚟/用來 + use/function predicates, but the evidence does not establish Canto Span’s full semantic resource class or all current exclusions. The runtime label remains under re-audit.
embedded.parser_representation_decision: Canto Span represents only the source-linked direct resource profile as internal ResourceUseLaiFunctionRelation while preserving IntendedFunctionRelation as the public compatibility label. The overt resource remains neutral between subject and topic, and 用, 嚟/來, and predicate material remain transparent.
embedded.parser_heuristic: Select the subtype only when an overt non-person resource precedes adjacent 用 + 嚟/來 and an overt action predicate, with no separate user, modal, copula, negator, or perfective actual-use material. The subtype inserts no hidden arguments and cannot license ordinary instrumental 用 or general purposive 嚟.

accounting.note: Five reviewed evidence units remain bounded. Runtime status metadata is aligned, but semantic resource/predicate scope and coordination remain unresolved; no Speaker A result; second-speaker work frozen.

reaudit.provisional_blockers: one independent Speaker A positive+comparison review; plain-language claim narrowed to current evidence
reaudit.productive_blockers: first-speaker review; second independent speaker frozen; semantic resource/predicate scope; coordination coverage; exact code-doc alignment
reaudit.next_action: Specify a narrower evidence-backed resource-function interface; Speaker A may be collected when convenient; keep Speaker B frozen.

authority.limitations: CP026 found one direct quote, one editorial interview-derived narrative, one published illustrative example, one purposive/conventionalization source, and one boundary source. Runtime status metadata is aligned in v0.5.184; semantic resource/predicate scope and coordinated predicate coverage remain unresolved. No speaker review; second-speaker work is frozen.
